DirectCause.name: DirectCause
DirectCause.label:
What was the DIRECT or specific cause of the problem?
What is the specific cause of the problem? Explain why the problem occurred (e.g. design/drawing error, manufacturing process, assembly/installation instructions):

Label13.name: Label13
Label13.label:
Why was the problem not DETECTED? How did it pass through to the customer?
Explain why the problem was not detected by the quality/inspection system (How did we have an escape?):

Label16.name: Label16
Label16.label:
How did the SYSTEM contribute to the problem? Did any business processes fail?
How was the problem introduced? Was there a failure or weakness in a process, procedure, work instruction or standard?

Label2.name: Label2
Label2.label: When identifying the root cause, please make sure to consider the following questions. These questions will be reviewed by the SCAR initiator.

Label4.name: Label4
Label4.label:
•	Have you identified a fundamental breakdown or failure of a process?
•	Is the identified root cause or solution applicable to that of other parts or processes?
•	Have you corrected the element of the process that caused the issue?
•	Have you ensured the escape will not reoccur?
•	Have you addressed the issue to the high standard UTC and customer will agree?

DetailedInvestigation.name: DetailedInvestigation
DetailedInvestigation.label: Detailed Investigation
DetailedInvestigation.tooltip: By selecting this field, a new section (3x5 Analysis) will become accessible.

FaultNotFoundDescription.name: FaultNotFoundDescription
FaultNotFoundDescription.label: Fault Not Found – Provide explanation and attach pictures/test report

Label19.name: Label19
Label19.label: Root causes for direct, detection, and systemic causes are required

Label20.name: Label20
Label20.label: CAUTION: OPERATOR ERROR WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED AS CAUSE

Rootcauseidentified.name: Rootcauseidentified
Rootcauseidentified.label: Has the root cause identified a fundamental breakdown or failure of a process?

Elementofprocess.name: Elementofprocess
Elementofprocess.label: Has the element of the process that caused the root cause been corrected?

OtherPartsorProcesses.name: OtherPartsorProcesses
OtherPartsorProcesses.label: Are the identified root cause and related corrective action applicable to other parts or processes?

OtherPart.name: OtherPart
OtherPart.label: If Yes, list other part numbers

CustomerCausedDescription.name: CustomerCausedDescription
CustomerCausedDescription.label: Customer-Caused – Provide explanation and attach pictures/test report

SystematicCause.name: SystematicCause
SystematicCause.label: SYSTEMATIC CAUSE: How did the product development/production/support processes contribute to the problem? (Examples: inadequate requirement flow down, manufacturing process planning, configuration management.)

DetectionCause.name: DetectionCause
DetectionCause.label: DETECTION CAUSE: How did the non-conformance escape the process? Why did the quality system fail to detect the non-conformance? (Examples: inadequate in-process/final inspection, testing, process control)

DirectCause1.name: DirectCause1
DirectCause1.label: DIRECT CAUSE: What is the root cause for the technical problem that caused the non-conformance? (Examples: Inadequate tooling, fixturing, gaging, handling, work instructions.)

children.caption: Fault Not Found – Provide explanation and attach pictures/test report
Customer-Caused – Provide explanation and attach pictures/test report
When identifying the root cause, please make sure to consider the following questions. These questions will be reviewed by the SCAR initiator.

•	Have you identified a fundamental breakdown or failure of a process?
•	Is the identified root cause or solution applicable to that of other parts or processes?
•	Have you corrected the element of the process that caused the issue?
•	Have you ensured the escape will not reoccur?
•	Have you addressed the issue to the high standard UTC and customer will agree?

What was the DIRECT or specific cause of the problem?
What is the specific cause of the problem? Explain why the problem occurred (e.g. design/drawing error, manufacturing process, assembly/installation instructions):

Why was the problem not DETECTED? How did it pass through to the customer?
Explain why the problem was not detected by the quality/inspection system (How did we have an escape?):

How did the SYSTEM contribute to the problem? Did any business processes fail?
How was the problem introduced? Was there a failure or weakness in a process, procedure, work instruction or standard?
Root causes for direct, detection, and systemic causes are required
CAUTION: OPERATOR ERROR WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED AS CAUSE
DIRECT CAUSE: What is the root cause for the technical problem that caused the non-conformance? (Examples: Inadequate tooling, fixturing, gaging, handling, work instructions.)

DETECTION CAUSE: How did the non-conformance escape the process? Why did the quality system fail to detect the non-conformance? (Examples: inadequate in-process/final inspection, testing, process control)

SYSTEMATIC CAUSE: How did the product development/production/support processes contribute to the problem? (Examples: inadequate requirement flow down, manufacturing process planning, configuration management.)
Has the root cause identified a fundamental breakdown or failure of a process?
Has the element of the process that caused the root cause been corrected?
Are the identified root cause and related corrective action applicable to other parts or processes?
If Yes, list other part numbers
